name: Izzy Friedman
description: American film score composer and saxophonist (1903-1981)
abstract: Irving "Izzy" Friedman (December 25, 1903 – November 21, 1981) was an American jazz reedist, best known for playing clarinet on records released by Paul Whiteman and Bix Beiderbecke in the 1920s.

about_quotes.speaker: Red Norvo
about_quotes.evidence: Izzy Friedman, Isaac Free- man, he used to play clarinet with Whiteman, who went to California and became oh, he contracted for all those -- the bands for all those musicals at MGM and all those things.

about_quotes.speaker: Armand Hug
about_quotes.evidence: Paul Morez invited Bix and Trumbauer and Izzy Friedman to play out to his house. So he invited us out too. So that was really a real great evening for me. We played together out there

about_quotes.speaker: Armand Hug
about_quotes.evidence: that's when we really got something real going there, you know, with Bix playing the trumpet, you know. And had Izzy Friedman playing clarinet.
